title: SEO analysis for 72 degrees
assignees: Chris Traynor, Clayton Coombs, Keith Bruss
description:
Hi,

This is inline with the call earlier. Tanya said they noticed a big decrease with their leads. 

Info from Tanya: 
  • Just for Oct 1st to Oct 27th [she removed today 28th since its not a full day yet] 
  • They dropped 607 vs 1295 last year. 
  • Looks like getting hit by 72degrees website [https://www.72degrees.com/] only 314 vs 1064  last year 
  • GBP leads also decreased 151 vs 240 last year

Holt itself leads is up 74 and 1 last year [new acquisition]

Thanks so much!

title: Pop up modal
description:
Holt reached out and sent the following this morning:

I made an update to the top utility bar this AM just to get something done quickly. The code snippet can be found here: views/partials/headers/default.blade.php, live example below. Feel free to update/override based on your team's best practices.


By chance do you guys have a simple pop-up modal script/file that can be added? If not, no need to rush anything since the giveaway ends this week.


Thank you! -Jordan

This is the client that makes changes in the theme files sometimes 😅 but at the same time he's pretty great to work with when we explain how things work. What can we do here? Is there a plugin or script we can use for them? I know the Holt site is difficult...

title: Local Map Ranking Concerns
description:
Hi Clayton,

Seeking help and follow up regarding the DM I sent last time about the Holt local map ranking concerns the client has. You were also tagged on the email. 

I have included the info from the email below:
 
Hope you're both doing well. I wanted to reach out and ask if you could take a quick look at Holt’s latest Local Search Grid report.

We’ve been keeping an eye on rankings for key map pack terms, and several have continued to slip this month. Queries like "Plumber near me," "Emergency plumber," and "Plumbing repair" are trending in the wrong direction, and visibility in surrounding grid points has taken a noticeable hit.

Would you mind reviewing the data and letting us know what you think might be driving this? If there are any technical or content-related updates we should prioritize, we’re all ears.

Thanks much!
